company.name: Evari
company.founderBackground: Product designer and filmmaker; grew up racing BMX, 20 years in architecture, product design, and visual effects.
exhibits: Salon Prive at Blenheim Palace (alongside McLaren Automotive, Bugatti, Ferrari)

oneLiner: British-designed, hand-built carbon-fibre electric bikes that bring road speed, gravel capability, and fully-integrated electronics into a single uncompromising all-road machine.

positioning.summary: Evari is a premium British e-mobility brand. Every 856 is hand-built in the UK from monocoque carbon fibre with aerospace-grade titanium components. Lights, motor, battery, computer and brake hoses are routed inside the frame so nothing breaks the silhouette. The bikes are the natural companion to a McLaren, a Riva, or a weekend in the Highlands — not a commodity.
pillars: Total electronic integration (no visible cables, no bolt-on clutter)
Sustainable mobility as a lifestyle upgrade
Sold like a luxury object, not a utility appliance
notFor: Budget commuters looking for a cheap e-bike

tone: Design-led — talks about materials, engineering and ride feel, not horsepower
Precise numbers over marketing adjectives (85 Nm, 17.8 kg, 90 km eco range)
Never shouty, never discount-led, never tech-bro
do: Lead with the object, not the feature list
Use craft language: hand-built, machined, integrated, bespoke
Reference the collaborators when relevant (Bosch, DT Swiss, Rohloff, Classified)
Write for an audience that already appreciates good things
dont: Don't use emoji in outbound copy
Don't lead with 'eco' — sustainability is implicit, not a sales pitch

family.finish: Core — solid gloss colours (Graphite Grey, Skandium White)
Entry to the family — solid gloss (Carmine Red)
family.audience: The first Evari — lowest barrier to entry

family.finish: Custom Studio — metallic gloss (Topanga Orange) in collaboration with Kustomflow
family.audience: The collector — colour + finish + components pushed further

family.transmission: Classified Powershift (dual-speed Bluetooth-controlled rear hub) layered on Rohloff
family.audience: The technologist — full gear range, wireless shifting
The adventurer — longer tours, gravel, UK backcountry

sharedSpecs.frame: Monocoque carbon fibre frame, fork, seatpost, rims, handlebars, stem
sharedSpecs.hardware: Aerospace-grade titanium bolts, snubber, and belt tensioner
sharedSpecs.motor: Bosch Performance Line CX mid-drive, 85 Nm
sharedSpecs.turbo: Up to 340% power boost in turbo
sharedSpecs.integration: Lights, motor, battery, computer display, and brake hoses all routed internally
sharedSpecs.buildLocation: Hand-built in the UK with UK-sourced components

collections.name: Core
collections.description: Pure Evari performance — the reference 856 in solid gloss colourways.

collections.name: Custom Studio (CS)
collections.description: Colour, finish, and components pushed further. Built in collaboration with Kustomflow.

collections.name: Bespoke
collections.description: One-of-one commissions — the ultimate expression of individuality.

primary.label: Design-conscious UK commuter (HNW)
primary.description: 35–60, London / Home Counties, earns well, already owns a road bike, values objects that look as good parked as they do ridden.

primary.label: Luxury lifestyle buyer
primary.description: Overlaps with supercar, yacht, watch, and art collectors. Discovered us at Salon Prive / Blenheim.

primary.label: Adventure tourer
primary.description: Buys the 856 EXP for multi-day UK + European touring. Wants titanium + carbon durability with long range.

primary.label: Design-forward professional (architect, creative director, product designer)
primary.description: Appreciates British industrial design. The bike is a daily object, not a hobby.

accountExamples: Private members' clubs (Soho House, 5 Hertford Street, The Conduit)
Luxury car dealerships (McLaren, Bentley, Aston Martin, Rolls-Royce)
Private UK medical clinics with HNW patient bases
Architecture and design studios in London
Specialist cycling retailers that already carry premium brands

differentiators: 100% hand-built in the UK with UK-sourced parts
Monocoque carbon + titanium construction (not aluminium, not bonded)
Total cable integration — nothing breaks the silhouette
Bosch CX drivetrain with 6 modes and 340% turbo
Classified Powershift option — Bluetooth wireless shifting

pricing.positioning: Premium — entry from ~£7,000 / ~$9,000 (856ST). Custom Studio and Bespoke on application.
pricing.tone: Never lead on price. Lead on object, then on build.

messagingAnchors: "The e-bike that begs to go faster." (third-party — Cycling Electric)
"Designed for riders who already own good things."

whenWritingCopy: Always call the product the '856' (never 'our e-bike' or 'our product')
Use British spelling (colour, tyre, centre, organisation)
Default location references to the UK unless the Play targets elsewhere
Reference the company name in full as 'Evari' on first mention, then 'the 856' or 'Evari' thereafter
whenSourcingProspects: Target HNW / luxury-adjacent audiences — not mass-market commuter or utility buyers
Geographic default: United Kingdom (London, Home Counties, Cotswolds, Highlands)
Consider co-location with existing luxury categories (supercars, yachts, watches, private members clubs)
Avoid bike shops as targets unless they are top-tier premium specialists
